title: stephanomeria exigua subsp. coronaria
intro: Stephanomeria exigua subsp. coronaria, commonly known as a species of the Stephanomeria genus, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is also known as the 'Crown Celosia' or 'Desert Celosia', and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are often used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues.
